metadata.title: Operation Ghost Water and Wastewater Systems Attack Scenario
metadata.author: Cybersecurity Analyst

overview.brief_summary: This attack scenario outlines the steps taken by the Operation Ghost group to compromise a water and wastewater system.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Credential Store Windows Service User Account File System Target Artifact (Tasks) Target Artifact (Application) Target Artifact (Alternate) Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Reg.exe Utility Response Metadata (Mechanism) Delivery Method Process Injection User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Code Injection SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the water and wastewater systems using artificial intelligence techniques.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers identify open ports and services on the system by monitoring network traffic.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers set up a command and control server by acquiring infrastructure over the internet.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2025-57819: Sangoma FreePBX contains an authentication bypass vulnerability due to insufficiently sanitized user-supplied data allows unauthenticated access to FreePBX Administrator leading to arbitrary database manipulation and remote code execution.
stage.description: The attackers trick employees into installing malware through fake emails.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2018-5430: TIBCO JasperReports Server contain a vulnerability which may allow any authenticated user read-only access to the contents of the web application, including key configuration files.
stage.description: The attackers execute malicious code within legitimate processes.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers change settings to allow remote access.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers escalate privileges using known vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.technique_name: T1198: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers discover operating system and installed software.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attackers use valid accounts to move laterally.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers transfer stolen data out of the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers cause disruption to the water supply.

Scenario.summary: The Operation Ghost group compromises a water and wastewater system by gathering information, setting up a command and control server, tricking employees into installing malware, executing malicious code, changing settings, escalating privileges, disabling antivirus software, guessing weak passwords, discovering operating system and installed software, moving laterally, collecting sensitive data, transferring stolen data, and disrupting the water supply.
